record.field:
The 12 English Tenses

English has 12 main tenses formed by combining 3 time frames (past, present, future) with 4 aspects (simple, continuous, perfect, perfect continuous).

1. Present Simple

Form: Subject + base verb (he/she/it adds -s)

Use: Habits, facts, schedules.  I work every day. She speaks English well.

Ukrainian: Теперішній простий час  |  Greek: Ενεστώτας απλός

2. Present Continuous

Form: Subject + am/is/are + verb-ing

Use: Actions happening now; temporary situations.  I am working right now.

Ukrainian: Теперішній тривалий час

3. Present Perfect

Form: Subject + have/has + past participle

Use: Past actions with present relevance; experience.  I have visited Paris.

Ukrainian: Теперішній перфектний час

4. Present Perfect Continuous

Form: Subject + have/has been + verb-ing

Use: Ongoing actions that started in the past.  I have been working here for 5 years.

5. Past Simple

Form: Subject + past tense (regular: -ed; irregular: see table)

Use: Completed actions at a specific past time.  She worked yesterday.

Ukrainian: Минулий простий час

6. Past Continuous

Form: Subject + was/were + verb-ing

Use: Actions in progress at a specific past moment.  I was sleeping when you called.

7. Past Perfect

Form: Subject + had + past participle

Use: Action completed before another past action.  She had already left when I arrived.

8. Past Perfect Continuous

Form: Subject + had been + verb-ing

Use: Action ongoing up to a point in the past.  He had been waiting for two hours.

9. Future Simple (will)

Form: Subject + will + base verb

Use: Predictions, spontaneous decisions, promises.  I will call you tomorrow.

Ukrainian: Майбутній простий час

10. Future Continuous

Form: Subject + will be + verb-ing

Use: Actions in progress at a specific future moment.  This time tomorrow I will be flying to London.

11. Future Perfect

Form: Subject + will have + past participle

Use: Action completed before a specific future point.  By Friday I will have finished the report.

12. Future Perfect Continuous

Form: Subject + will have been + verb-ing

Use: Duration of an action up to a future point.  By June she will have been studying English for 10 years.

Quick Reference Table

AspectPastPresentFuture
Simpleworkedwork/workswill work
Continuouswas workingam/is/are workingwill be working
Perfecthad workedhave/has workedwill have worked
Perf. Cont.had been workinghave/has been workingwill have been working

Common Irregular Verbs

Irregular verbs do not follow the standard -ed pattern. Memorise these common forms.

BasePast SimplePast ParticipleUkrainian
bewas/werebeenбути
beginbeganbegunпочинати
breakbrokebrokenламати
bringbroughtbroughtприносити
buildbuiltbuiltбудувати
buyboughtboughtкупувати
catchcaughtcaughtловити
choosechosechosenвибирати
comecamecomeприходити
dodiddoneробити
drinkdrankdrunkпити
drivedrovedrivenводити
eatateeatenїсти
fallfellfallenпадати
feelfeltfeltвідчувати
findfoundfoundзнаходити
flyflewflownлітати
forgetforgotforgottenзабувати
getgotgot/gottenотримувати
givegavegivenдавати
gowentgoneіти
havehadhadмати
hearheardheardчути
keepkeptkeptтримати
knowknewknownзнати
leaveleftleftзалишати
loselostlostпрогравати
makemademadeробити
meetmetmetзустрічати
paypaidpaidплатити
readreadreadчитати
runranrunбігти
saysaidsaidговорити
seesawseenбачити
sellsoldsoldпродавати
sendsentsentвідправляти
sitsatsatсидіти
sleepsleptsleptспати
speakspokespokenговорити
spendspentspentвитрачати
standstoodstoodстояти
taketooktakenбрати
teachtaughttaughtвчити
telltoldtoldрозповідати
thinkthoughtthoughtдумати
understandunderstoodunderstoodрозуміти
winwonwonвигравати
writewrotewrittenписати

Tip: Group by pattern — buy/bought/bought, bring/brought/brought, catch/caught/caught all share -ought.

English Articles

English has three article types: indefinite (a/an), definite (the), and zero article (no article).

A / An — Indefinite Article

Use with singular countable nouns when referring to any one of a group or introducing something new.

  • a before consonant sounds: a book, a university, a European
  • an before vowel sounds: an apple, an hour, an MBA

Ukrainian: Немає прямого відповідника; передається контекстом.

The — Definite Article

When to use theExample
Already mentionedI saw a dog. The dog was huge.
Unique objectsthe sun, the moon, the Earth
Superlativesthe best, the tallest building
Rivers, seas, mountain rangesthe Nile, the Alps, the Pacific
Countries with plural/republic namesthe UK, the USA, the Netherlands

Zero Article (∅)

No article with: plural/uncountable nouns in general statements, proper names, languages, meals, sports, transport phrases.

Dogs are friendly. She speaks English. We had dinner. He plays football. by car, by train.

Common Mistakes

WrongCorrectRule
I go to the school.I go to school.Institutions in general purpose
She plays the tennis.She plays tennis.Sports → zero article
I like the music.I like music.Uncountable in general

English Conditionals

Conditionals express "if" situations. There are four main types.

Zero Conditional — General Truths

Form: If + present simple, present simple

If you heat water to 100°C, it boils.  If I miss the bus, I walk.

Ukrainian: Якщо нагріти воду до 100°C, вона закипає.

First Conditional — Real Future Possibility

Form: If + present simple, will + base verb

If it rains, I will stay at home.  If you study hard, you will pass.

Ukrainian: Якщо піде дощ, я залишуся вдома.

Second Conditional — Unreal Present/Future

Form: If + past simple, would + base verb

If I had a million dollars, I would travel the world.

Use were (not was) with all persons in formal English: If she were here…

Ukrainian: Якби в мене був мільйон доларів, я б подорожував світом.

Third Conditional — Unreal Past

Form: If + past perfect, would have + past participle

If I had studied harder, I would have passed.  If she had left earlier, she wouldn't have missed the train.

Ukrainian: Якби я більше вчився, я б склав іспит.

Quick Summary Table

TypeIf-clauseMain clauseUse
Zeropresent simplepresent simpleGeneral truth
Firstpresent simplewill + baseReal possibility
Secondpast simplewould + baseUnreal/hypothetical
Thirdpast perfectwould have + ppUnreal past

Modal Verbs

Modal verbs express ability, permission, obligation, possibility, and advice. Followed by a bare infinitive.

Can / Could

ModalMeaningExampleUkrainian
canability (present)I can swim.вміти / могти
canpermission (informal)Can I open the window?можна?
couldability (past)She could run fast as a child.міг/могла
couldpolite requestCould you help me?чи не могли б ви?
couldpossibilityIt could be true.може бути

May / Might

mayformal permission / ~50% possibilityYou may leave. It may rain.можете / можливо
mightweaker possibility (~30%)It might rain.може бути

Must / Have to

muststrong obligation (internal)I must finish this today.мусити / повинен
mustdeduction (certainty)She must be tired.напевно
have toobligation (external)I have to wear a uniform.треба / необхідно
must notprohibitionYou must not smoke here.не можна / заборонено
don't have tono obligationYou don't have to come.не обов'язково

Should / Would

shouldadvice / recommendationYou should see a doctor.слід / варто
wouldconditional / polite request / past habitI would help if I could. Would you like tea? We would visit her every summer.б / чи не хотіли б / бувало

Greek equivalents

  • can → μπορώ (boró)
  • must → πρέπει (prépi)
  • should → θα έπρεπε
  • may/might → μπορεί (borei) / ίσως (ísōs)

Passive Voice

The passive voice focuses on the action or object rather than the agent (doer).

Formation: Subject + be (conjugated) + past participle [+ by + agent]

TenseActivePassive
Present SimpleThey make cars here.Cars are made here.
Past SimpleSomeone stole my bike.My bike was stolen.
Future SimpleThey will announce results.Results will be announced.
Present PerfectThey have built a road.A road has been built.
Present ContinuousThey are fixing the roof.The roof is being fixed.

Ukrainian: Пасивний стан — Будинок був збудований.   Greek: Παθητική φωνή — Το βιβλίο γράφτηκε.

Reported Speech

Reported speech conveys what someone said without direct quotation. Tenses usually shift back one level.

Tense Shifts

DirectReported
"I work" (present simple)he said he worked
"I am working" (present cont.)he said he was working
"I worked" (past simple)he said he had worked
"I will come" (future)he said he would come
"I can help" (can)he said he could help

Time and Place Shifts

DirectReported
todaythat day
yesterdaythe day before
tomorrowthe next day
here / this / nowthere / that / then

Key Reporting Verbs

  • admit — He admitted that he was wrong.
  • deny — She denied taking the money.
  • suggest — He suggested going to the cinema.
  • promise — She promised to call.
  • warn — He warned me not to touch it.

Ukrainian: Він сказав, що він працює. / Вона запитала, чи можу я допомогти.
